refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and ties it explicitly to customer-defined limits and network conditions, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and verify it remains within the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant system components.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.
DP2: Use dedicated high-reliability network link with QoS prioritization.
DP3: Integrate packet loss monitoring and dynamic adjustment algorithms.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.

coupling_check.notes: The adaptive communication protocol logically isolates teleoperation communication quality management, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk of increased latency due to retransmissions impacting teleoperation responsiveness.
Assumes error correction algorithms are compatible with existing network infrastructure.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding latency monitoring as a complementary FR to address potential responsiveness issues.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation control, communication management, and related protocols.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data packets over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to report communication quality metrics such as packet loss to the NGHE Solution module.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands and task instructions.

module_containment_scores.notes: High diffusion risk if teleoperation communication logic is embedded here; less containment of teleoperation-specific logic.
Poor containment; vehicle module should focus on physical control, not communication protocols.
Best containment and lowest diffusion risk; aligns with ownership of teleoperation communication.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring, not communication protocol management.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning the DP to the Teleoperation module centralizes communication quality management, minimizing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Network Link interface stability.
Risk of insufficient integration with Safety Services for safety-critical alerts related to communication failures.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Establish clear interface contracts with Safety Services for communication failure alerts.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (adaptive communication protocol) directly addresses the refined FR with clear logical mapping and measurable performance.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as DP is contained within Teleoperation module.
Potential interface complexity with Network Link and Telemetry interfaces.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment scores and aligns well with DP responsibilities.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring packet loss metrics via Telemetry interface and testing under network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to Teleoperation module with defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Add explicit interface for communication failure alerts to Safety Services.
Consider latency as an additional FR to complement packet loss.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition is sufficient with clear FR refinement, DP selection, and IM assignment meeting containment and verification criteria. No immediate need to zigzag or revise at this level.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant system components.

revise.why: Current artifacts meet requirements and architectural clarity.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Teleoperation module has capability to implement adaptive protocols.
open_risks: Interface integration with Safety Services for safety alerts.
evidence_needed: Test results showing packet loss under various network conditions.
Interface validation between Teleoperation and Safety Services.
